annotations.label: Connected space

anchor.snippet: a connected space is a topological space that cannot be represented as the union of two or more disjoint non-empty open subsets

annotations.note: `ConnectedSpace` is defined as a nonempty `PreconnectedSpace`, matching the article's definition.

annotations.label: Connected subset

anchor.snippet: A subset of a topological space

annotations.note: `IsConnected s` formalizes a connected subset as a nonempty preconnected subset.

annotations.label: Disconnected / connected space

anchor.snippet: is said to be disconnected if it is the union of two disjoint non-empty open sets

annotations.note: Connected/disconnected dichotomy is encoded via `ConnectedSpace`/`PreconnectedSpace` and `IsPreconnected`.

annotations.label: Connectedness equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: Connectedness defines an equivalence relation in the following sense

annotations.note: `connectedComponentSetoid` is the equivalence relation "belongs to the same connected component".

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for connectedness
annotations.note: Several characterizations exist (`isPreconnected_iff_subset_of_disjoint`, clopen forms, `preconnectedSpace_iff_clopen`), but not collected as one bundled proposition.

annotations.label: Union of connected sets containing a common point is connected

anchor.snippet: the union of any collection of connected subsets such that each contains

annotations.note: `isPreconnected_sUnion` shows a family of preconnected sets sharing a common point has preconnected union.

annotations.label: Connected component of a point

anchor.snippet: The connected component of a point

annotations.note: `connectedComponent x` is the union of all preconnected sets containing `x`.

annotations.label: Connected components of a space

anchor.snippet: The maximal connected subsets

annotations.note: `ConnectedComponents α` is the quotient by `connectedComponentSetoid`.

annotations.label: Components form a partition

anchor.snippet: The components of a topological space

annotations.note: Disjointness of distinct components and covering by `mem_connectedComponent` give the partition; expressed as the quotient `ConnectedComponents`.

annotations.label: Every component is closed

anchor.snippet: Every component is a closed subset of the original space

annotations.note: `isClosed_connectedComponent` proves `IsClosed (connectedComponent x)`.

annotations.label: Components of the rationals are singletons

anchor.snippet: the connected components of the set of the rational numbers are the one-point sets

annotations.note: `ℚ` is shown to be a `TotallyDisconnectedSpace`, i.e. all components are singletons.

annotations.label: Quasi-component contains component

anchor.snippet: be the intersection of all clopen sets containing

annotations.note: The connected component is contained in the intersection of all clopen neighborhoods (the quasi-component).

annotations.label: Totally disconnected space

anchor.snippet: A space in which all components are one-point sets is called totally disconnected

annotations.note: `TotallyDisconnectedSpace` matches the Wikipedia definition (only singletons are preconnected).

annotations.label: Totally separated space

anchor.snippet: is called totally separated if, for any two distinct elements

annotations.note: `TotallySeparatedSpace` is defined via `IsTotallySeparated` on the universe.

annotations.label: Totally separated implies totally disconnected

anchor.snippet: Clearly, any totally separated space is totally disconnected, but the converse does not hold

annotations.note: Instance promoting `TotallySeparatedSpace` to `TotallyDisconnectedSpace`.

annotations.label: Two copies of rationals glued away from zero

anchor.snippet: take two copies of the rational numbers

annotations.note: No grep hits for this particular gluing construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed interval is connected

anchor.snippet: The closed interval

annotations.note: `isConnected_Icc` (and `isPreconnected_Icc`) prove `Icc a b` is connected.

annotations.label: Union of two disjoint open intervals is disconnected

anchor.snippet: The union of

annotations.note: Not stated as a standalone example/lemma in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Indiscrete topology is connected

anchor.snippet: with the indiscrete topology is connected

annotations.note: No specific `ConnectedSpace` instance for the indiscrete (top) topology found.

annotations.label: Subspace not connected

anchor.snippet: The subspace

annotations.note: Specific subspace example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Convex subset of Euclidean space is connected

anchor.snippet: A convex subset of

annotations.note: `Convex.isConnected` shows any nonempty convex set in a real TVS is connected.

annotations.label: Euclidean plane minus origin

anchor.snippet: A Euclidean plane excluding the origin

annotations.note: Mathlib proves that in a real normed space of rank > 1 the complement of any point is path connected, generalizing the plane-minus-origin example.

annotations.label: Plane with a line removed

anchor.snippet: A Euclidean plane with a straight line removed is not connected

annotations.note: No specific lemma about plane-minus-line being disconnected found.

annotations.label: Real line is connected

anchor.snippet: the space of real numbers with the usual topology, is connected

annotations.note: `Real.instPathConnectedSpace` plus the `PathConnectedSpace.connectedSpace` instance give connectedness of `ℝ`.

annotations.label: Sorgenfrey line is disconnected

anchor.snippet: The Sorgenfrey line is disconnected

annotations.note: Sorgenfrey line is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Removing points from Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: If even a single point is removed from

annotations.note: In normed spaces of rank > 1, removing a point still leaves a path-connected (hence connected) space.

annotations.label: Topological vector space is simply connected

anchor.snippet: Any topological vector space

annotations.note: Path-connectedness of a TVS is formalized; simple connectedness of a generic TVS is not given as a single instance though contractibility of star-shaped/convex sets is.

annotations.label: Discrete space with two elements is disconnected

anchor.snippet: Every discrete topological space with at least two elements is disconnected

annotations.note: Discrete spaces are totally separated; the specific "nontrivial discrete is disconnected" statement isn't isolated.

annotations.label: Spectrum of discrete valuation ring

anchor.snippet: the spectrum of a discrete valuation ring consists of two points and is connected

annotations.note: No specific lemma about Spec of a DVR being a connected two-point space.

annotations.label: Cantor set is totally disconnected

anchor.snippet: The Cantor set is totally disconnected

annotations.note: `Mathlib.Topology.Instances.CantorSet` proves compactness/closedness but not total disconnectedness.

annotations.label: Homotopy equivalence preserves connectedness

anchor.snippet: is homotopy equivalent to a connected space

annotations.note: Homotopy equivalence preservation is proved for simple connectedness, but a direct version for plain connectedness isn't stated.

annotations.label: Topologist's sine curve

anchor.snippet: The topologist's sine curve is an example of a set that is connected but is neither path connected nor locally connected

annotations.note: Topologist's sine curve is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General linear group components

anchor.snippet: The general linear group

annotations.note: No statement on connected components of `GL_n` found.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for connected spectrum

anchor.snippet: The spectra of commutative local ring and integral domains are connected

annotations.note: No specific lemma about connectedness of `Spec` of local rings / integral domains found.

annotations.label: Plane with infinite line deleted

anchor.snippet: An example of a space that is not connected is a plane with an infinite line deleted from it

annotations.note: Not formalized as a worked example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Path in a topological space

anchor.snippet: A path from a point

annotations.note: `Path x y` is a structure extending `C(I, X)` with endpoints `x` and `y`.

annotations.label: Path-component and path-connected space

anchor.snippet: A path-component of

annotations.note: `pathComponent` and `PathConnectedSpace` define path-components and path-connected spaces.

annotations.label: Path-component is maximal path-connected subset

anchor.snippet: Equivalently, a path-component of

annotations.note: `IsPathConnected.subset_pathComponent` together with `isPathConnected_pathComponent` give maximality.

annotations.label: Path-connected implies connected

anchor.snippet: Every path-connected space is connected

annotations.note: `PathConnectedSpace.connectedSpace` is a (priority 100) instance from path-connected to connected.

annotations.label: Connected subsets of real line are intervals

anchor.snippet: Subsets of the real line

annotations.note: `isPreconnected_iff_ordConnected` characterizes preconnected subsets of a conditionally complete linear order as the order-connected (interval) sets.

annotations.label: Arc-connected space

anchor.snippet: is said to be arc-connected or arcwise connected if any two topologically distinguishable points can be joined by an arc

annotations.note: Arc-connectedness (joining by an embedded arc) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arc-component

anchor.snippet: An arc-component of

annotations.note: No arc-component definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hausdorff path-connected implies arc-connected

anchor.snippet: Every Hausdorff space that is path-connected is also arc-connected

annotations.note: Not formalized since arc-connectedness is absent in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Line with two origins is path-connected but not arc-connected

anchor.snippet: An example of a space which is path-connected but not arc-connected is given by the line with two origins

annotations.note: Line with two origins is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Locally connected at a point

anchor.snippet: is said to be locally connected at a point

annotations.note: Mathlib has the global notion `LocallyConnectedSpace`; pointwise local connectedness isn't a separate definition.

annotations.label: Characterization of locally connected

anchor.snippet: if and only if every component of every open set

annotations.note: `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connectedComponentIn_open` characterizes local connectedness via components of open sets being open.

annotations.label: Locally path-connected

anchor.snippet: is said to be locally path-connected if it has a base of path-connected sets

annotations.note: `LocPathConnectedSpace` defines locally path-connected spaces.

annotations.label: Open subset of locally path-connected: connected iff path-connected

anchor.snippet: An open subset of a locally path-connected space is connected if and only if it is path-connected

annotations.note: `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected` is the exact statement.

annotations.label: Locally connected but not connected

anchor.snippet: A simple example of a locally connected (and locally path-connected) space that is not connected

annotations.note: No dedicated example formalizing this counterexample.

annotations.label: Topologist's sine curve not locally connected

anchor.snippet: A classic example of a connected space that is not locally connected is the so-called topologist's sine curve

annotations.note: Topologist's sine curve is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Intersection of connected sets need not be connected

anchor.snippet: The intersection of connected sets is not necessarily connected

annotations.note: This is a non-implication/counterexample remark not formalized.

annotations.label: Union of connected sets need not be connected

anchor.snippet: The union of connected sets is not necessarily connected

annotations.note: Counterexample-style statement not formalized.

annotations.label: Conditions under which union of connected sets is connected

anchor.snippet: This means that, if the union

annotations.note: `IsPreconnected.union`/`IsConnected.union` give the union-with-common-point case.

annotations.label: Set difference and connectedness

anchor.snippet: The set difference of connected sets is not necessarily connected

annotations.note: Non-implication remark, not formalized.

annotations.label: Main theorem of connectedness

anchor.snippet: Main theorem of connectedness

annotations.note: `IsConnected.image`/`IsPreconnected.image` formalize that the continuous image of a connected set is connected.

annotations.label: Path-connected implies connected

anchor.snippet: Every path-connected space is connected

annotations.note: Instance promoting `PathConnectedSpace` to `ConnectedSpace`.

annotations.label: Open connected sets in locally path-connected spaces are path-connected

anchor.snippet: In a locally path-connected space, every open connected set is path-connected

annotations.note: `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected` directly states this equivalence.

annotations.label: Locally path-connected implies locally connected

anchor.snippet: Every locally path-connected space is locally connected

annotations.note: Anonymous `instance : LocallyConnectedSpace X` at LocPathConnected.lean:170 derives local connectedness from local path-connectedness.

annotations.label: Locally path-connected: path-connected iff connected

anchor.snippet: A locally path-connected space is path-connected if and only if it is connected

annotations.note: `pathConnectedSpace_iff_connectedSpace` for `LocPathConnectedSpace` is the stated equivalence.

annotations.label: Closure of connected set is connected

anchor.snippet: The closure of a connected subset is connected

annotations.note: `IsConnected.closure`/`IsPreconnected.closure` formalize closure preservation.

annotations.label: Connected components are closed

anchor.snippet: The connected components are always closed

annotations.note: `isClosed_connectedComponent` proves each component is closed.

annotations.label: Components of locally connected space are open

anchor.snippet: The connected components of a locally connected space are also open

annotations.note: `isOpen_connectedComponent` (and `isClopen_connectedComponent`) under `[LocallyConnectedSpace α]`.

annotations.label: Components are disjoint unions of path-components

anchor.snippet: The connected components of a space are disjoint unions of the path-connected components

annotations.note: `biUnion_connectedComponent_pathComponent_eq` expresses each connected component as a union of path-components.

annotations.label: Quotient preserves connectedness

anchor.snippet: Every quotient of a connected

annotations.note: `Quotient.instConnectedSpace` (and `Surjective.connectedSpace` for continuous surjections) covers this.

annotations.label: Product of connected spaces is connected

anchor.snippet: Every product of a family of connected

annotations.note: Instances at Basic.lean:710,714 prove `ConnectedSpace (∀ i, X i)` and `ConnectedSpace (α × β)`.

annotations.label: Open subset of locally connected is locally connected

anchor.snippet: Every open subset of a locally connected

annotations.note: `IsOpen.locallyConnectedSpace` shows open subspaces inherit local connectedness.

annotations.label: Manifolds are locally path-connected

anchor.snippet: Every manifold is locally path-connected

annotations.note: `ChartedSpace.locPathConnectedSpace` derives local path-connectedness for charted spaces whose model is locally path-connected.

annotations.label: Arc-wise connected implies path-connected

anchor.snippet: Arc-wise connected space is path connected

annotations.note: Arc-connectedness isn't formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuous image of arc-wise connected is arc-wise connected

anchor.snippet: Continuous image of arc-wise connected set is arc-wise connected

annotations.note: Arc-connectedness is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: 5-cycle graph

anchor.snippet: The 5-cycle graph

annotations.note: `cycleGraph n` is defined; specific connectedness of `cycleGraph 5` is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Connective spaces

anchor.snippet: there is a category of connective spaces consisting of sets with collections of connected subsets satisfying connectivity axioms

annotations.note: Connective spaces (Muscat–Buhagiar) are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph connectedness via topology

anchor.snippet: every graph can be canonically made into a topological space

annotations.note: The canonical topology on a graph linking graph- and topological-connectedness isn't formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperconnected implies connected

anchor.snippet: If there exist no two disjoint non-empty open sets in a topological space

annotations.note: Hyperconnected = irreducible; Mathlib's `PreirreducibleSpace.preconnectedSpace` instance gives the implication.

annotations.label: Simply connected implies connected

anchor.snippet: Since a simply connected space is, by definition, also required to be path connected

annotations.note: An anonymous priority-100 instance `SimplyConnectedSpace X → PathConnectedSpace X` (SimplyConnected.lean:68) composes with `PathConnectedSpace.connectedSpace`.

annotations.label: Contractible implies path connected

anchor.snippet: Every contractible space is path connected and thus also connected

annotations.note: Anonymous priority-100 instance at Contractible.lean:108 turns `ContractibleSpace` into `PathConnectedSpace`.

annotations.label: Deleted comb space

anchor.snippet: The deleted comb space furnishes such an example

annotations.note: Deleted comb space isn't in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Well-chained space

anchor.snippet: A well-chained space is a metric space in which two arbitrary points can be connected by a chain of points that are arbitrarily close

annotations.note: Well-chained spaces (epsilon-chained metric spaces) aren't formalized in Mathlib.
